attacking_style: Pure time trial specialist who dominates flat and rolling ITTs with raw power, averaging 53-56kph. Does not attack in road stages; used as pace-setter and domestique for team leaders.
team_tactics: Deployed as high-powered tempo domestique day after TT wins, riding destructively on 14-16% climbs. Used for breakaway control on transition stages and course reconnaissance for GC leaders. Ineos burns him as super-domestique despite ITT dominance.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling ITTs (8-35km) where pure wattage dominates. Technical corners and steep climbing (>10%) expose weight disadvantage. Home roads provide measurable psychological advantage (10+ second margins).
vulnerability: Technical courses with tight corners erode pure power advantage—beaten by 7s on 9km technical UAE ITT. Gets dropped on mountain stages despite brief GC competitiveness. Occasionally overpaces in challenging conditions (gravel near-crash).
key_patterns: Won 5+ consecutive Giro ITTs (2020-2021), unbeaten in Grand Tour time trials during peak
Regularly beats world champions by 16-28 seconds on flat courses, resetting power benchmarks
Deployed as destructive tempo rider day after ITT victories, exceptional recovery capacity
Nearly wins bunch sprints from domestique role, raw power threatens pure sprinters unexpectedly
Technical courses reduce margins significantly: only 7s loss on 9km vs 24-28s wins on flat
Maintains form across disciplines: chases breakaways day after climbing Jebel Jais, TTs after mountain stages
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta showed peak form—56kph TT win beating Evenepoel, destructive climbing work next day, near sprint victories. Exceeds pure TT specialist expectations.
